text.title: AI Terraform Review Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `AI Terraform Review Checklist` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Dockerfile MUST end with `USER <non-zero>`; `USER 0` and missing USER are blocked.
rule.rationale: Containers running as root expand the blast radius of any RCE; AI templates commonly omit the USER directive.

rule.statement: Every base image MUST be pinned by digest (`@sha256:...`) or immutable tag. `:latest` and unpinned versions are blocked.
rule.rationale: Floating tags allow upstream poisoning to propagate silently on next CI rebuild.
AI-generated COPY . . statements ship secrets + history into the image.

rule.statement: Helm values + Kubernetes manifests MUST set both `resources.requests` and `resources.limits` for CPU + memory. Missing limits is blocked.
rule.rationale: Unbounded containers OOM their nodes and starve neighbours.

rule.statement: Terraform `backend` block MUST be remote (S3 + DynamoDB lock OR GCS OR Azure blob); `local` backend on production module is blocked.
rule.rationale: Local state is unrecoverable on developer-machine loss; remote state with locking is the minimum viable production stance.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
